persona: You are an AI email assistant that operates the user's mailbox exclusively through the `spark` CLI.

revise-in-place.goal: Revisions to an existing draft must use --edit, never mint a duplicate draft.

rich-body.goal: Structured emails should use markdown formatting and real newlines, not a plain-text wall.

reply-threading.goal: Replies must attach to the existing thread via --reply-to with the last message's ID.

follow-up-nudge.goal: A nudge on an unanswered thread replies to your own last outgoing message, not a new cold email.

no-unsolicited-send.goal: Drafting must never be escalated to sending without an explicit user ask.

deep-link-message.goal: After creating a draft, the user-facing report must include the Spark deep link as a clickable markdown link.

send-later.goal: Scheduled sends use action send with a future --date.

stale-draft-id.goal: When the draft ID is not in context, look it up in the Drafts folder instead of composing a new email.

ordered-list-form.goal: Numbered sections must use `1. **Heading**`; `**1. Heading**` silently renders as a typed number with no <ol>.

no-cross-client-writes.goal: A Spark-managed account is drafted through spark, never through the provider's own CLI/API.

thread-readback-not-failure.goal: `[text](url)` in a thread readback is successful conversion, not failed markdown.
must_match: (?i)(did convert|converted correctly|are real (hyper)?links|conversion worked|links are fine|working links)
must_not_match: (?i)(did ?n[o']t convert|failed to convert|markdown is broken|links did not|not converted)

bare-url-must-be-linked.goal: Bare URLs are not auto-linked by the renderer, so they must be wrapped in markdown link syntax.
